vectors.note: three informed legal half-moves: the full chain is selected

Alice/Bob: first plays Ra1-a4 (informed); second had blind-premoved two anterior candidates for their slot — an illegal Ke8-e6 (earliest) and a legal Ke8-e7. K=1: only the earliest anterior (illegal) is considered, forgiven (skipped); the legal correction is ignored, so the slot reverts to the player and the chain stops at the first move

first plays step 1 (informed @100) and step 2 (a premove @110, attested before second's reply @200 — anterior to its slot 3); second answers step 1 @200; the interleaved chain consumes all three, the step-2 premove applying as a forgiving anterior selection

first plays Ra1-a4; second then plays an informed illegal move (Ke8-e6, attested after the anchor): the chain stops there and the illegal informed ply is not included (the arbiter renders illegalmove against its signer)
a third half-move attested after the cutoff is excluded; the chain stops at the second
first plays; the opponent never fills their slot, so the chain stops at one half-move (the slot stays unfilled and the on-move player's clock runs — adjudication resolves it)
